truth.papers.A30.title: Dynamic recrystallization mechanisms of an Fe–8% Al low density steel under hot rolling conditions
truth.papers.A30.paper_scope: Investigation of dynamic recrystallization mechanisms (CDRX and DDRX) in Fe-8% Al low-density ferritic steel under hot torsion conditions (900-1100°C, 0.1-10 s^-1) and their effect on texture and roping defect.

prediction.papers.A30.title: Dynamic recrystallization mechanisms of an Fe–8% Al low density steel under hot rolling conditions
prediction.papers.A30.paper_scope: Experimental study of the effects of strain, strain rate and temperature on dynamic recrystallization mechanisms (continuous CDRX versus discontinuous DDRX) in an Fe-8%Al ferritic steel during hot torsion deformation, with emphasis on microstructure, texture evolution and implications for roping resistance.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe8al.alloys_notes: Non-ordered A2 lattice (BCC solid solution); density approximately 7 g cm⁻³; supplied as fully recrystallized plate with equiaxed grains.

truth.processes.proc_fe8al_initial.description: As-received material: hot rolled to cumulative deformation of 1, fully recrystallized with equiaxed grains of ~800 μm.
truth.processes.proc_fe8al_torsion.description: Hot torsion tests performed in temperature range 900-1100°C and strain rate range 0.1-10 s^-1 under argon atmosphere, followed by immediate argon quench (0.5 s, cooling rate ~200 °C s^-1) to retain microstructure.

prediction.processes.proc_fe8al_initial.description: As-received material hot rolled in three successive passes with cumulative deformation equal to 1, leading to a fully recrystallized, morphologically isotropic microstructure.
prediction.processes.proc_fe8al_torsion.description: Hot torsion test in the DDRX regime at 1100°C and 10 s⁻¹ under argon atmosphere, followed by immediate argon quench.
prediction.processes.proc_fe8al_torsion.processes_n: Strain ε = 2. Observations near the surface (0.9 radius).

truth.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_initial.pr: Hot rolled to cumulative deformation ε=1, fully recrystallized.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_initial.pr: Three successive hot rolling passes; cumulative deformation equal to 1; fully recrystallized equiaxed initial microstructure with grain size ~800 μm.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-: Strain ε = 1; strain rate 0.1 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).
Strain ε = 2; strain rate 0.1 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).
Strain ε = 5; strain rate 0.1 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).
Strain ε = 25; strain rate 0.1 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_1100C_5s-1: Strain ε = 2; strain rate 5 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe8al_1100C_10s-: Strain ε = 2; strain rate 10 s⁻¹; argon atmosphere; quenched immediately (0.5 s); initial cooling rate ~200 °C/s; analysis near surface (0.9 radius).

truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e1.o: Elongated original grains with subgrain formation near serrated grain boundaries
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e2.o: Elongated original grains with progressive subgrain network formation and increase in HAB fraction, onset of CDRX
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e5.o: Aggregate of crystallites bounded partly by LABs and partly by HABs, no distinction between original and recrystallized grain boundaries
Predominantly recrystallized fine-grained structure with high fraction of HABs from CDRX
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_5s-1_e2.ov: Mixture of small equiaxed dislocation-free DDRX grains and elongated deformed grains with subgrains
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_5s-1_e2.mi: mixture of equiaxed new grains and elongated old grains
low dislocation density in new grains; high in old grains
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_10s-1_e2.o: Mixture of small equiaxed DDRX grains and elongated deformed grains, similar to 5 s^-1 condition
truth.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_10s-1_e2.m: equiaxed new grains and elongated old grains
low dislocation density in new grains; high in old grains

prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_initial.overall_: Fully recrystallized single-phase ferrite with equiaxed grains; morphologically isotropic
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e1.o: Original ferrite grains elongated in the shear direction; subgrains form preferentially close to serrated initial grain boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e1.m: elongated original grains with subgrains near boundaries
progressive increase moderated by dynamic recovery
original grains elongated in shear direction; subgrains preferentially near initial grain boundaries which exhibit serrations
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e2.o: Original grains more elongated along shear direction; subgrain network invades grain interiors; selective fragmentation begins
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e2.m: elongated original grains progressively subdivided by subgrain boundaries
increased due to strain hardening, partially annihilated by dynamic recovery
selective fragmentation: some grains more fragmented than others; volume fraction of HABs strongly increases
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e5.o: Intermediate structure between subgrain and grain aggregates; initial grain boundaries indistinguishable from new boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1000C_01s-1_e5.m: aggregate of crystallites bounded partly by LABs and partly by HABs
organized into boundaries subdividing original grains
crystallites delimited partly by low-angle and partly by high-angle boundaries; original grains fully consumed
Balanced subgrain and grain boundaries; HABs partly remainders of original elongated/broken-up boundaries (GDRX)
fine recrystallized-like structure with remaining broken-up original boundaries
D2 {112}<111> is the strongest component at large strains
HABs and LABs in balance; GDRX-induced boundary pinch-off
balance between subgrain and grain boundaries; serrated original boundaries elongated and broken up
D2 {112}<111> strongest under negative shear; maximal intensity reportedly at ε = 15
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_5s-1_e2.ov: Bimodal mixture of small equiaxed recrystallized grains free of substructure and large elongated original grains with internal misorientations
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_5s-1_e2.mi: mixture of young small equiaxed grains and old large elongated grains
very low intensity, close to random
low in recrystallized grains, higher in old deformed grains
bimodal: small equiaxed recrystallized grains alongside large elongated original grains
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_10s-1_e2.o: Bimodal mixture of small equiaxed recrystallized grains free of substructure and large elongated original grains with internal misorientations
prediction.structures.sample_fe8al_1100C_10s-1_e2.m: mixture of young small equiaxed grains and old large elongated grains
very low intensity, close to random
low in recrystallized grains, higher in old deformed grains
bimodal: small equiaxed recrystallized grains alongside large elongated original grains

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_10: Microstructural characterization, grain/subgrain boundary analysis, orientation mapping, texture measurement

Progressive transformation of subgrain boundaries (LABs) into grain boundaries (HABs) during CDRX; misorientation distribution shows increase in HAB fraction with strain. At ε=25, balance between LABs and HABs.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_11: Characterization of DDRX microstructure and texture

DDRX produces small equiaxed dislocation-free grains with random orientations; misorientation distribution close to MacKenzie distribution; high strain rate suppresses dynamic recovery, leading to nucleation and growth of new grains.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_in: Microstructure observation and orientation mapping of the as-received material
Fully recrystallized equiaxed ferrite with ~800 μm grain size. Black lines indicate HABs (≥15°) and red lines indicate LABs (2°–15°).
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_10: Microstructure and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1000°C, 0.1 s⁻¹, ε = 1
Original grains elongated in shear direction; subgrains form preferentially near serrated initial grain boundaries. Misorientation distribution dominated by angles below 15°.
Microstructure and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1000°C, 0.1 s⁻¹, ε = 2
Subgrain network progressively invades original grain interiors; selective fragmentation; volume fraction of HABs strongly increases.
Microstructure and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1000°C, 0.1 s⁻¹, ε = 5
Intermediate structure of crystallites bounded partly by LABs and partly by HABs; original grain boundaries no longer distinguishable.
Microstructure, texture and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1000°C, 0.1 s⁻¹, ε = 25
Balance between subgrain and grain boundaries; GDRX contributes via break-up of elongated original boundaries. D2 {112}<111> is the strongest texture component under negative shear.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe8al_11: Microstructure, texture and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1100°C, 5 s⁻¹, ε = 2

DDRX activated: mixture of small equiaxed recrystallized grains without substructure and large elongated original grains with internal misorientations. Misorientation distribution close to MacKenzie distribution with maximum near 45°. {110} pole figure maximum intensity 1.77 m.r.d.
Microstructure, texture and misorientation analysis after hot torsion at 1100°C, 10 s⁻¹, ε = 2

DDRX activated: mixture of small equiaxed recrystallized grains without substructure and large elongated original grains with internal misorientations. Misorientation distribution close to MacKenzie distribution with maximum near 45°. {110} pole figure maximum intensity 1.66 m.r.d.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The average subgrain size increases with decreasing strain rate or increasing temperature and varies linearly with the inverse of the yield stress (no specific values reported).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 3: {1 1 0} pole figures of Fe–8% Al deformed by torsion show typical bcc torsion texture; strongest component D2: {-1-12}<111> is self-symmetric; maximum intensity of D2 observed at ε=15.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Figure 7: Strain rate–temperature map showing DDRX activation domain: 900°C/10 s^-1, 1000°C/10 s^-1, 1100°C/10 s^-1; DDRX not activated at lower strain rates (e.g., 900°C/1 s^-1, 1000°C/0.5 s^-1, 1100°C/0.1 s^-1).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Roping defect reduction: To limit roping (inhomogeneous orientation distribution), it is advantageous to promote DDRX to achieve recrystallized grains with random orientations.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The initial grain size of the material is ~800 μm equiaxed; material is morphologically isotropic.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Geometric dynamic recrystallization (GDRX) mechanism is also discussed: HABs at large strains partly originate from elongation and pinch-off of original boundaries, possibly operating concurrently with CDRX.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Stress-strain curves are not shown but the text mentions up to the maximum of the stress–strain curve for strain hardening and dynamic recovery balance.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: At high temperature and high strain rate, the Fe-8%Al ferritic alloy undergoes discontinuous dynamic recrystallization (DDRX), producing randomly oriented grains that are advantageous for limiting roping defects.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: At lower temperature and strain rate, continuous dynamic recrystallization (CDRX) occurs, associated with progressive transformation of subgrain boundaries into grain boundaries and development of the D2 {112}<111> texture component under negative shear.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Geometric dynamic recrystallization (GDRX) operates simultaneously with CDRX at large strains, where original high-angle boundaries become elongated and broken up ('pinch-off') when their thickness is sufficiently reduced.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Strain rate–temperature map (Figure 7) delineating DDRX activation domain: at 900°C DDRX activates at 10 s⁻¹ (not at 1.0 s⁻¹); at 1000°C DDRX activates at 10 s⁻¹ (not at 0.5 s⁻¹); at 1100°C DDRX activates at 10 s⁻¹ (not at 0.1 s⁻¹).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The average subgrain size in the CDRX regime increases with decreasing strain rate or increasing temperature and varies linearly with the inverse of the yield stress.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Proposed DDRX nucleation mechanisms include accelerated rotation of a subgrain, coalescence of adjacent subgrains, or local migration of a grain boundary into the interior of a neighbouring grain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The maximal intensity of the D2 {112}<111> texture component in the CDRX regime is observed at ε = 15.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: In the DDRX regime, recrystallized grains exhibit {110} pole figure maximum intensities of 1.77 m.r.d. at 5 s⁻¹ and 1.66 m.r.d. at 10 s⁻¹, indicating very weak texture close to random.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Surface roping defects after deep drawing are attributed to inhomogeneous orientation distribution of individual grains; favouring DDRX during hot rolling is proposed to obtain randomly oriented recrystallized grains and reduce roping.
